channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 5 Docker Scenarios Every Developer Should Practice (With Fixes & Best Practices)
item.description: So you know the basics of Docker — docker run, docker build, maybe some docker compose up. But do you know what happens when things break? This guide walks you through 5 real-world Docker...

item.title: Pygame Snake, Pt. 1
item.description: Pygame is a module designed to allow us to make 2D games using Python. I am just learning pygame myself. I've always loved the game Snake, and I find that it makes a good learning project, too....

item.title: What's in ShipKit's $249 Next.js starter
item.description: I have started a lot of Next.js apps. Every single one burns the same first two months: auth, payments, database setup, CMS, email templates, UI components. Different project, same plumbing....

item.title: 15 Things I Learned in Tech (That Actually Matter)
item.description: There’s a point where engineering stops being about writing correct code and starts being about making irreversible decisions under uncertainty. At that stage, the hardest problems aren’t: syntax...

item.title: How to Triage a Phishing Alert Faster — Without Rebuilding the Process Every Time
item.description: Most phishing alerts do not take long because they are difficult. They take long because the workflow is inconsistent. You get the alert. A user reported a suspicious email. Maybe your mail...

item.title: What 1,000+ Developer Posts Told Me About the Biggest Pain Points Right Now
item.description: I built a tool that reads Hacker News, Dev.to, and Stack Exchange every day and looks for complaints. Not discussions, not tutorials - complaints. The places where developers say "this is broken"...

item.title: feel i invaded half the world just by coding!
item.description: every one had anything like app, website and after months even years of work you look at the country analytics seeing half the world is blue suddenly you feel a very tasty feeling reminding me of...

item.title: 20. Node.js
item.description: BootCamp by Dr.Angela 1. What is Node.js? Node.js is a JavaScript runtime that allows you to run JavaScript outside the browser (on a server or local machine) Why use frameworks/libraries? : Reuse...

item.title: Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
item.description: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

item.title: SQL Window Functions and CTEs
item.description: You can do quite a lot with some of the most popular SQL queries such as SELECT, WHERE, and JOIN. But there exists some question that you cannot answer with these queries such as What is each...

item.title: Please Don’t Throw That Away
item.description: Why making yard sales easier could save the planet There is an unfortunate truth that we all live with in America that many of us don’t even see. Walk into any house in America and you’ll find...

item.title: ISS astronauts are getting new laptops
item.description: Even astronauts need to level up their laptops once in a while - including the crew of Expedition 74 on board the ISS, which NASA announced last week is in the process of some computer upgrades....

item.title: Tim Cook was an innovator — just not the Jobs kind
item.description: Under Steve Jobs, Apple released the groundbreaking products that defined the company. But the company wouldn't be what it is today without Tim Cook's reign of ruthless efficiency. Jobs' legacy...

item.title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
item.description: Founded by an OSU researcher, the startup is developing AI agents that can become experts in any domain.

item.title: AI backlash is coming for elections
item.description: Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI...

item.title: ChatGPT’s new Images 2.0 model is surprisingly good at generating text
item.description: ChatGPT Images 2.0, the newest image-generation model from OpenAI, shows just how much AI capabilities have evolved over the last few years.

item.title: OpenAI Beefs Up ChatGPT’s Image Generation Model
item.description: The ChatGPT Images 2.0 model is here. Our testing shows it’s better at creating more detailed images and rendering text, but it still struggles with languages other than English.

item.title: OpenAI’s updated image generator can now pull information from the web
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out the latest version of its AI-powered image generator with new "thinking capabilities," allowing it to search the web to help it create multiple images from a single prompt....

item.title: Sam Altman throws shade at Anthropic’s cyber model, Mythos: ‘fear-based marketing’
item.description: This week, during a podcast appearance, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called out his competitor's new cybersecurity model, noting that the company was using fear to make its product sound more impressive...

item.title: Framework’s Laptop 13 Pro launch event
item.description: Framework CEO Nirav Patel is showing off his company’s latest modular, repairable laptops in San Francisco today. The headliner is the new Laptop 13 Pro, which is its first machine fully machined...

item.title: Mozilla Used Anthropic’s Mythos to Find and Fix 271 Bugs in Firefox
item.description: The Firefox team doesn’t think emerging AI capabilities will upend cybersecurity long term, but they warn that software developers are likely in for a rocky transition.

item.title: X makes it 1,900 percent more expensive to post links
item.description: Posting links to X through custom social media software just got a lot more expensive. On Monday, X significantly increased how much it costs to post a URL via the X API, which is what third-party...

item.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
item.description: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. According to new research...

item.title: Framework announces Laptop 13 Pro, ‘the MacBook Pro for Linux users’
item.description: Every time we review a Framework laptop, we find familiar pros and cons. They're truly upgradable, incredibly repairable, but we always wish the battery lasted longer. We always wish the build...

item.title: Framework’s first eGPUs turn its laptop into a desktop PC
item.description: Remember when Framework made the first laptop where you can easily upgrade its entire internal video card in three minutes flat? The company's getting into the external graphics game, too. As...

item.title: Framework is building a better couch keyboard because everyone hates the Logitech one
item.description: If you have a wireless keyboard with a touchpad that lets you control your PC from across the room, chances are it's a Logitech K400. Framework CEO Nirav Patel is betting that you hate using it -...

item.title: The AirPods are Tim Cook’s most underrated achievement
item.description: Apple Silicon chips. The iPhone's dominance. Apple Vision Pro. During Tim Cook's 15-year tenure as Apple CEO there were highs - and in the case of the Vision Pro, maybe a low - that helped define...

item.title: Tim Cook’s Legacy Is Turning Apple Into a Subscription
item.description: The soon-to-exit Apple CEO went all in on services. Now, the incoming CEO, John Ternus, will need to embrace the AI era.

item.title: Building agent-first governance and security
item.description: As AI agents increasingly work alongside humans across organizations, companies could be inadvertently opening a new attack surface. Insecure agents can be manipulated to access sensitive systems...

item.title: Clarifai deletes 3 million photos that OkCupid provided to train facial recognition AI, report says
item.description: The photo deletion comes after an FTC settlement with Clarifai. The company had asked OkCupid — whose executives had invested in Clarifai — to share data in 2014, according to court documents.
